cases.goal: Create arithmetic.py with add, subtract, multiply, and divide functions. Add a thorough standard-library unittest suite. Division by zero must raise ZeroDivisionError.
success_criteria: All four arithmetic operations return correct results for integers and floats.
Division by zero raises ZeroDivisionError.
The automated test suite passes.

cases.goal: Create report.json containing exactly the object {"total": 42, "items": [7, 14, 21]} as valid JSON.
success_criteria: report.json is valid JSON.
The decoded value exactly matches the requested object.

cases.goal: Write SECURITY.md with concise sections named Threat model, Trust boundaries, and Residual risks. Each section must contain at least one concrete item.
success_criteria: SECURITY.md contains all three required section headings.
Every required section contains concrete non-empty content.
verification_commands: python3 -c "from pathlib import Path; s=Path('SECURITY.md').read_text(); assert all(('# ' + x in s or '## ' + x in s) for x in ['Threat model','Trust boundaries','Residual risks'])"

cases.goal: Create sales.csv with the rows north,12; south,8; north,5 and a summarize.py program that writes totals.json with totals grouped by region.
success_criteria: sales.csv contains the requested source rows.
Running summarize.py creates valid JSON with north=17 and south=8.
verification_commands: python3 summarize.py && python3 -c "import json; assert json.load(open('totals.json')) == {'north': 17, 'south': 8}"

cases.goal: Create slugify.py. As a CLI it must convert its text arguments to a lowercase ASCII slug, collapse separators, and print the result.
success_criteria: The CLI lowercases and joins words with single hyphens.
Punctuation and repeated whitespace do not create repeated separators.
The module can be imported without executing the CLI.
verification_commands: python3 -c "import slugify; assert slugify.slugify('  Hello,   LOOP Agent! ') == 'hello-loop-agent'" && test "$(python3 slugify.py 'Hello, LOOP Agent!')" = hello-loop-agent

cases.goal: Create config_merge.py with a deep_merge(base, override) function. Nested dictionaries merge recursively, lists and scalars are replaced, and neither input is mutated.
success_criteria: Nested dictionaries merge recursively.
Lists and scalar values are replaced by the override.
The input dictionaries remain unchanged.
verification_commands: python3 -c "from config_merge import deep_merge; a={'db':{'host':'x','ports':[1]},'debug':False}; b={'db':{'ports':[2]},'debug':True}; r=deep_merge(a,b); assert r=={'db':{'host':'x','ports':[2]},'debug':True}; assert a['db']['ports']==[1] and b['db']['ports']==[2]"

cases.goal: Create workflow.py implementing pending -> running -> completed or failed transitions. Invalid transitions must raise ValueError and terminal states cannot transition again.
success_criteria: All declared valid transitions succeed.
Invalid transitions raise ValueError.
Completed and failed are terminal.

cases.goal: Create report.html containing a titled accessible table with columns Name and Score and rows Ada=98, Linus=91, Grace=95. Include a caption and valid document structure.
success_criteria: The document has html, head, title, and body elements.
The table has a caption, two column headers, and exactly the three requested data rows.
Every requested name and score is present.
verification_commands:
python3 - <<'PY'
from html.parser import HTMLParser
from pathlib import Path
class P(HTMLParser):
 def __init__(s):super().__init__();s.tags=[];s.items=[];s.rows=[];s.row=None;s.x=None
 def handle_starttag(s,t,a):
  s.tags.append(t)
  if t=='tr':s.row=[]
  elif t in ('title','caption','th','td'):s.x=[t,'']
 def handle_data(s,d):
  if s.x:s.x[1]+=d
 def handle_endtag(s,t):
  if s.x and t==s.x[0]:
   x=(t,' '.join(s.x[1].split()));s.items.append(x)
   if t in ('th','td'):assert s.row is not None;s.row.append(x)
   s.x=None
  if t=='tr':s.rows.append(s.row);s.row=None
p=P();p.feed(Path('report.html').read_text())
assert {'html','head','title','body','table','caption'}<=set(p.tags)
m=[v for t,v in p.items if t in ('title','caption')]
assert len(m)==2 and all(m)
assert p.rows==[[('th','Name'),('th','Score')],[('td','Ada'),('td','98')],[('td','Linus'),('td','91')],[('td','Grace'),('td','95')]]
PY

cases.goal: Create events.jsonl with events login/alice, purchase/alice, login/bob and summarize_events.py that writes summary.json counting events by type and unique users.
success_criteria: events.jsonl contains exactly the three requested events as JSON Lines.
The generated summary counts login=2 and purchase=1.
The generated summary reports two unique users.
verification_commands: python3 summarize_events.py && python3 -c "import json; assert json.load(open('summary.json')) == {'events': {'login': 2, 'purchase': 1}, 'unique_users': 2}"

cases.goal: Create retry.py with retry(operation, attempts). It returns on success, retries failures up to the exact attempt limit, rejects attempts below one, and re-raises the last exception.
success_criteria: A successful operation returns immediately.
A failing operation is called exactly attempts times and the final exception is re-raised.
attempts below one raises ValueError.

cases.goal: Create indexer.py with build_index(documents), mapping normalized words to sorted document ids without duplicates. Ignore punctuation and case.
success_criteria: Words are normalized case-insensitively and punctuation is ignored.
Each posting list is sorted and contains no duplicate document ids.
The requested example produces the exact expected index.
verification_commands: python3 -c "from indexer import build_index; assert build_index({'b':'Hello, loop loop.','a':'HELLO world'}) == {'hello':['a','b'],'loop':['b'],'world':['a']}"

cases.goal: Create string_utils.py with is_palindrome and count_words plus a standard-library unittest suite covering normal, empty, punctuation, and mixed-case inputs. count_words must count alphanumeric word tokens and keep contractions such as Don't as one word.
success_criteria: is_palindrome ignores punctuation, whitespace, and case.
count_words counts alphanumeric word tokens and keeps contractions as one word.
The unittest suite covers edge cases and passes.
verification_commands: python3 -m unittest discover -v && python3 -c "from string_utils import is_palindrome,count_words; assert is_palindrome('A man, a plan, a canal: Panama!') and not is_palindrome('Loop agent'); assert count_words(\"Don't stop believing\")==3 and count_words('Hello, WORLD! 42')==3 and count_words('')==0"
